$schema_comment: A complete design profile. design-analyst returns a spec referencing tokens defined here; main agent generates code using only those tokens.
one_line: Monochromatic with one vibrant accent. Inter throughout. Geometric, dense, precise. Feels like Linear, Vercel, Raycast, Arc, or Cursor.

background.role: Page background. Near-black with a hint of blue. NOT pure #000.

surface_subtle.role: Inset surfaces (input fields when not focused, code blocks).

text_primary.role: Body text, headlines. Slightly warm white, never pure #FFF.

text_secondary.role: Supporting text, metadata, labels, helper text.

border.role: Default dividers, input borders. Very subtle.

border_strong.role: Stronger borders (focused input, hover on cards).

accent.role: Linear-signature purple-blue. Used SPARINGLY — primary buttons, key links, focus rings. Never as background fill for large surfaces.

accent_subtle.role: Accent at 12% opacity for backgrounds (selected row, active tab indicator strip).
Accent at low opacity for backgrounds.

rules: Dark mode is the DEFAULT for this profile. Implement light mode only if asked
Never use pure black (#000) or pure white (#FFF). Always the slight-warmth/coolness variants
Accent is for PRIMARY buttons, key links, focus rings, active-state indicators. Not for decoration, not for backgrounds of large surfaces
Default to surface (not background) for cards and panels — gives the subtle depth this aesthetic needs
Semantic colors (success, warning, danger) are USED RARELY. A green dot is enough — never paint a whole banner green for 'connected'

display.rule: Inter Display is Inter at large sizes with optical sizing. Modern browsers handle this automatically.

mono.family: Geist Mono, Berkeley Mono, JetBrains Mono, ui-monospace, monospace
mono.rule: Use mono LIBERALLY for this profile. Keyboard hints (⌘K), IDs, timestamps — anything that's not prose. It's a signature of the look.

scale.$rule: Base is 14px, not 16px — this profile is denser. Bumps to 16 only for long-form reading surfaces.

rules: Inter for EVERYTHING except code/mono use cases. Don't mix in serifs
Tabular numbers (font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums) on any numeric column or stat
Headlines: weight 600, letter-spacing -0.025em. Anything looser looks marketing-y, wrong for product UI
Line-height: 1.5 for prose, 1.3 for UI labels, 1.1 for large display sizes
Use ALL CAPS sparingly — only for tiny labels (xs, weight 500, letter-spacing 0.05em)
DENSE. Default to 1.0× what feels right, sometimes 0.75×
Between sidebar items: 2-4px
Between form fields: 16px (not 24px — too loose for this aesthetic)
Section gutters: 24-32px (vs editorial-premium's 96-128px)
Padding inside buttons/inputs: 8px 12px (small) or 10px 14px (default), not 12px 24px
Default radius: md (6px). Same as editorial — neither aesthetic is pill-shaped
Inputs and buttons: md (6px)
Cards/panels: lg (8px)
Modals/dialogs: xl (12px) — this is the one place to go larger
Never pill buttons on rectangles. Avatars and chips are obviously full radius
Use the surface-on-surface hierarchy, not shadows. background → surface → surface_active
Shadows allowed for modals/popovers only, and they're SHARP: 0 0 0 1px border, 0 8px 24px rgba(0,0,0,0.4)
Never use shadows on cards in the main content area — borders carry the weight
1px borders are the primary depth tool. Two adjacent surfaces are distinguished by border, not shadow

button_primary.rule: Primary button uses the accent color. Single primary button per visible area — having two on screen at once is a profile violation

input_text.rule: Focus state: border becomes accent, plus a 3px accent-at-12% glow. NOT the browser default orange ring

keyboard_hint.rule: Use these LIBERALLY. ⌘K, ESC, Tab, ↵ everywhere it makes sense. A signature of this look.

login.structure: Single centered card on a near-black background. Logo + headline + form + OAuth. Max-width ~400px. No marketing copy panel, no testimonials — this profile assumes the user already knows your product.
do: OAuth buttons above email (fast path for known users)
Logo as a small mono-style mark, not a big illustration

dashboard.structure: Fixed left sidebar (240-280px) with sections + items, main content area on the right. Top toolbar with breadcrumb, search (⌘K hint), user menu. Content area uses cards with 1px borders, no shadows.
do: Mono font for IDs, timestamps, any technical detail
Keyboard shortcut hints next to action items
Dense rows (32-40px tall) — not Airbnb-style 64px+ rows
dont: Tooltip prose explaining UI (the UI should be self-evident)
Stat cards with huge numbers in accent color

marketing_landing.structure: This profile works for marketing pages aimed at developers/technical audiences (Linear's marketing site is an example). For non-technical audiences, prefer editorial-premium.
do: Hero with the product itself as the visual (screenshot or animated UI)
Mono accents in copy ('Press ⌘K to start')
Dense feature grid, not a single hero claim

animations.library: gsap or CSS transitions — both fit
animations.philosophy: Micro-interactions only. Product UIs shouldn't have marketing motion. Tight, fast, snappy.

patterns.hover: Background color 150ms ease-out. Cursor pointer. Subtle border-color shift on cards.
patterns.button_press: scale(0.98) on active state, 80ms. Releases instantly.
patterns.list_reorder: GSAP Flip plugin for smooth list reordering — keeps the technical-precision feel.

tailwind_config_snippet.$comment: Drop-in extension. Default to dark mode by setting darkMode: 'class' and applying 'dark' to <html>. Or omit dark mode entirely and just use these as the base.
